mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are indistinguishable from baseline when INDPRO and HOUST move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising consumer price indices compress real equity valuations by elevating discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn positive in more than 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 210.0, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth signals strengthening aggregate demand, which directly improves corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines for two consecutive months, supply chain disruptions and capacity constraints compress corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO drops below its 3-month MA, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment combined with contracting real money supply indicates liquidity-driven job growth rather than organic expansion, which typically precedes equity drawdowns and yields negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive Rank IC over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower when PAYEMS rises and RSAFS falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary monetary policy regime, low federal funds rates paired with elevated unemployment trigger flight-to-safety flows that dominate equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single FEDFUNDS-only signal in out-of-sample testing.
